questions.question: Explain how the diagonal of a square can be used to construct a square with double the area.
questions.answer: The diagonal divides the square into two congruent right triangles. A new square constructed using the diagonal as its side has an area exactly twice that of the original square.

questions.question: Find the hypotenuse of a right triangle whose sides are 6 cm and 8 cm.
questions.answer: Using Pythagoras theorem: c² = 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100, therefore c = 10 cm.

questions.question: Show that (5,12,13) is a Pythagorean triple.
questions.answer: 5² + 12² = 25 + 144 = 169 and √169 = 13. Hence it satisfies a² + b² = c².

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a square whose side is 7 cm.
questions.answer: The diagonal of a square is a√2. Therefore diagonal = 7√2 cm.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a rectangle whose sides are 9 cm and 12 cm.
questions.answer: d² = 9² + 12² = 81 + 144 = 225, therefore d = 15 cm.

questions.question: Explain the concept of primitive Pythagorean triples with an example.
questions.answer: Primitive Pythagorean triples are triples of integers satisfying a² + b² = c² that have no common factor other than 1. Example: (3,4,5).

questions.question: Find the missing side if the hypotenuse is 13 cm and one side is 5 cm.
questions.answer: Other side² = 13² − 5² = 169 − 25 = 144, so side = 12 cm.

questions.question: Explain how the areas of squares on the sides of a right triangle are related.
questions.answer: The area of the square constructed on the hypotenuse equals the sum of the areas of the squares constructed on the other two sides.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a square whose side is 10 cm.
questions.answer: Diagonal = 10√2 cm.

questions.question: Find the hypotenuse of a triangle whose sides are 7 cm and 24 cm.
questions.answer: c² = 7² + 24² = 49 + 576 = 625, therefore c = 25 cm.

questions.question: Explain what scaled Pythagorean triples are.
questions.answer: Scaled triples are obtained by multiplying each number of a primitive Pythagorean triple by the same integer.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a rectangle with sides 8 cm and 15 cm.
Explain the significance of the Pythagoras theorem in geometry.
questions.answer: The theorem helps determine unknown sides of right triangles and calculate distances and diagonals in geometric shapes.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a square whose side is 12 cm.
questions.answer: Diagonal = 12√2 cm.

questions.question: Verify that (8,15,17) forms a Pythagorean triple.
Find the missing side if the hypotenuse is 17 cm and one side is 8 cm.
questions.answer: Other side² = 17² − 8² = 289 − 64 = 225, so side = 15 cm.

questions.question: Explain why the diagonal of a square forms a right triangle.
questions.answer: The diagonal divides the square into two equal right triangles because the angles of the square are 90°.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a rectangle with sides 12 cm and 16 cm.
Explain the importance of Pythagorean triples in mathematics.
questions.answer: They help identify right triangles and simplify calculations involving integer side lengths.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a square whose side is 14 cm.
questions.answer: Diagonal = 14√2 cm.

questions.question: Find the hypotenuse if the sides of a triangle are 9 cm and 40 cm.
questions.answer: c² = 9² + 40² = 81 + 1600 = 1681, therefore c = 41 cm.

questions.question: Explain the geometric interpretation of the Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: The theorem states that the area of the square built on the hypotenuse equals the sum of the areas of the squares built on the other two sides.

questions.question: Find the diagonal of a rectangle whose sides are 15 cm and 20 cm.
Explain how the theorem is useful in construction work.
questions.answer: Builders use the theorem to check right angles and measure diagonal distances in structures.

questions.question: Explain how the Pythagoras theorem is used to calculate distances on a map.
questions.answer: Distances between two points can be calculated by forming a right triangle and applying the Pythagoras relation.
